exemplar.title: To what extent does Starbucksâs focus on sustainable sourcing and waste reduction initiatives impact brand loyalty among environmentally conscious consumers?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Starbucksâs focus on sustainable sourcing and waste reduction initiatives impact brand loyalty among environmentally conscious consumers?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and coherent introduction with sufficient background context for Starbucksâ sustainability focus
Research question is explicitly stated, well-focused, and drives the structure of the investigation
Use of a generally appropriate range of secondary sources with some evidence of informed selection
weaknesses: Occasional long or fragmented sentences in the introduction reduce clarity
Discussion sometimes drifts into loosely related topics (e.g., logo history) rather than maintaining strict focus
Methodology lacks deeper justification of source reliability and contains no primary data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work clearly communicates the topic and provides adequate explanation (band 3-4). The research question is well formulated and maintained throughout (band 3-4). The methodology shows generally relevant source selection but without strong rationale or primary research (band 3-4). Overall performance aligns with the upper end of the 3-4 band, warranting a score of 4.

strengths: Source material is regularly cited and mostly relevant to support claims about sustainability and loyalty
Subject knowledge of CSR, brand identity, and industry tools is clear and coherent
Key terminology (e.g., âtriple bottom lineâ, âpremium pricingâ) is used correctly in context
weaknesses: Several citations are superficial or over-reliant on corporate press releases rather than critical academic sources
Sections sometimes lapse into narrative description rather than analytical integration of theory
Minor inaccuracies in mathematical notation (e.g., mis-labelled equation) interrupt the flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of source material and subject knowledge consistently fall within the 3-4 band: mostly relevant and appropriate but not consistently critical. Terminology is generally accurate but not at the precision required for the top band. The overall performance merits a mid-band score of 3.

strengths: Research is clearly relevant to the research question and draws on diverse industry and consumer data
Analysis is coherent, with a logical use of frameworks (Porterâs Five Forces, perceptual map) to support conclusions
Argument is well developed, balancing discussion of identity, competition, and customer engagement
weaknesses: Porterâs analysis and other tools are applied descriptively in places without deeper critical interrogation
Quantitative data (e.g., participation rates, price-sensitivity metrics) are referenced but not thoroughly analyzed
Evaluation of methodological limitations and broader implications is brief and underdeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The criterion performance fits the 7-9 band: good, relevant research; clear analysis; and a well-developed argument with partially critical evaluation. While the work demonstrates solid critical thinking, gaps in deeper interrogation and evaluation prevent entry into the highest band. A high-mid band score of 8 is therefore appropriate.

strengths: Structure follows EE conventions with logical headings, pagination, and an organized table of contents
Consistent font, heading hierarchy, and a clear word count/title page enhance readability
Works cited list and in-text citations are present and largely follow a coherent style
weaknesses: Minor formatting inconsistencies remain (broken URLs, occasional spacing anomalies)
Some duplicated or misaligned headings and OCR artifacts in footnotes interrupt the text flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall presentation meets all top-band descriptors for structure and formatting. Though there are minor inconsistencies, the conventions are correctly applied throughout. These strengths justify the maximum score of 4 in this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly phrased, sufficiently narrow (impact of two specific initiatives on one consumer segment) and is carried through the essayâs sections (brand identity, competitive landscape, customer engagement).  Nonetheless, parts of the discussion drift into general competition or logo history that are only loosely tied back to the RQ, showing partialânot completeâfocus.  This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band; because the question itself is clear and largely drives the structure, the higher mark of 4 is justified.

An argument is developed through the three main sections (identity, competition, customer engagement) and a balanced conclusion claims a âmedium extentâ of impact.  Some evaluation of limitations and competitor initiatives is present, yet it is brief and occasionally uncritical (e.g., assuming loyalty growth stems mainly from sustainability).  The discussion therefore fits the descriptor âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluationâ, meriting 4 marks.

The essay demonstrates sound understanding of CSR, brand identity, loyalty, and Porter's Five Forces, and applies them to Starbucks.  Explanations are generally accurate, yet some sections lapse into narrative (logo history, composting benefits) rather than analytical use of theory.  Source integration is patchy, so knowledge is clear but not fully synthesised.  This aligns with the middle band, earning 3 marks.

Key terms such as âbrand loyaltyâ, âperceptual mapâ, âpremium pricingâ, âtriple bottom lineâ and âsupplier bargaining powerâ are used correctly and in appropriate contexts.  Minor errors (e.g., mis-labelled equations, occasional informal phrasing) prevent consistent precision, but overall usage is mostly accurate.  Therefore the strand fits the 3-4 band at 3 marks.

Analytical tools (Porterâs Five Forces, perceptual map) are applied, and links between sustainability actions and loyalty are attempted.  However, many sections remain descriptive, and numerical evidence (e.g., loyalty-programme growth) is not thoroughly interrogated.  Conclusions drawn from Reddit comments or logo colours are only partially supported.  Thus the analysis is âclearâ but not consistently well-supported, matching the lower end of the 4-6 band (4 marks).

Source material (Starbucks reports, WWF data, Deloitte survey, Reddit posts, academic texts) is regularly cited and woven into paragraphs to underpin statements about sustainability and loyalty.  However, several citations are superficial or serve merely as factual inserts, and there is occasional over-reliance on corporate press releases.  The relevance is mostly clear, but application is not consistently critical.  This fulfils the âmostly relevant and appropriateâ description, warranting 3 marks in the 3-4 band.

The methodology section lists secondary sources (articles, reports, Reddit) and explains their general purpose, showing some informed selection.  The range is acceptable for a desk-based EE, but primary data are absent and the reasoning for source reliability or triangulation is minimal.  These limitations keep the work from the top band, yet the presence of varied, mostly relevant sources meets the âgenerally relevantâ descriptor.  A mid-band mark of 3 is most appropriate.

The student gathers a reasonable breadth of secondary data (corporate sustainability reports, industry statistics, consumer surveys, competitor information) that are evidently relevant to the RQ.  Although primary research is absent, the compiled evidence is appropriate and clearly linked to the investigation, satisfying the âgood research clearly relevantâ descriptor.  A solid placement near the middle of the 4-6 band (5 marks) reflects this quality.

Consistent font, heading hierarchy, citation superscripts, and a works-cited list demonstrate sound formatting.  A clear word count and title are present.  Minor formatting inconsistencies (some broken URLs, equation artefacts, and occasional spacing anomalies) exist, but overall conventions are observed.  Hence the work meets the criteria for correct application, earning 3 marks in this band.

The student clearly introduces Starbucks, states the sustainability focus, and explicitly restates the full research question in the introduction.  The context (mission, CSR priorities, specific initiatives) provides sufficient background for readers to understand why the topic matters.  While some explanatory sentences are fragmented and occasionally digressive, the communication of the topic itself is still accurate, coherent and more than merely âadequateâ; it is, however, not as polished or fully comprehensive as expected for the top band.  Hence the work meets the descriptors for the 3-4 band and merits the upper mark of 4.

The essay follows the required EE structure (title page with word count, table of contents, introduction, methodology, analysis sections, conclusion, works cited).  Headings are logical and pagination appears correct.  Minor issuesâsuch as some duplicated headings and occasional page-break errorsâprevent full marks, but the structure clearly meets expectations, justifying 3 marks within the top band.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and well-focused, providing strong guidance to the investigationâs scope and structure.
The inclusion of a precise word count and a descriptive title on the cover page meets formatting requirements and helps orient the reader.
Table of contents is present but the alignment and sizing of entries reduce readability. Consider standardizing spacing and font styles.
The introductory paragraph provides useful background but contains long, fragmented sentences. Break into smaller units to improve clarity and flow.
Footnote formatting shows OCR artifacts (e.g., stray tags). Clean these to ensure consistent citation style throughout.
The methodology section lists varied sources but lacks justification of their reliability and absence of primary data. Add rationale for selection and consider primary input.
This heading âTheory usedâ indicates a logical structure for theoretical framework and aligns well with EE requirements.
The equation \(\frac{7}{1}\) appears mis-labelled and unexplained. Ensure all mathematical notation is accurate and contextualized.
The background information is well-organized and shows coherent subject knowledge about sustainability commitments.
The logo history discussion is largely descriptive. To deepen analysis, link color symbolism and design changes more critically to consumer perceptions.
The perceptual map tool is explained but no figure is included. Insert a visual map to support your analysis of brand positioning.
Consider quantifying consumer price sensitivity (e.g., survey data) to strengthen the discussion on premium pricing and greenwashing perceptions.
âCompetitive landscapeâ is clearly labeled, aiding the logical flow between analysis sections.
When describing waste-reduction programs like âBorrow a Cup,â include actual participation or usage figures to substantiate the initiativeâs impact.
The definition of brand loyalty is precise and uses appropriate terminology in context.
The application of Porterâs Five Forces is appropriate and correctly described, showing clear understanding of the tool.
In the competition analysis, include specific sustainability metrics from Dunkinâ and Costa to substantiate comparisons.
Clarify how C.A.F.E. Practices concretely lower supplier bargaining power with quantitative examples.
The summary of Porterâs analysis is descriptive; strengthen by critically evaluating how Starbucksâ initiatives outperform competitors.
Customer Engagement section is well signaled, creating a clear shift to consumer-focused analysis.
Cite the customer retention statistics from a primary source or recent academic study rather than a corporate press release.
The Reddit feedback sample needs scrutiny for bias; discuss representativeness and potential limitations of social-media data.
The conclusion restates main points but lacks evaluative commentary on broader implications and methodological constraints.
Works Cited list shows varied styles. Adopt a single citation format (e.g., MLA or APA) and apply consistently with hanging indents.
Reddit citation lacks proper capitalization and punctuation in the title. Verify each entry against style guidelines.
Citation list on page 17 shows inconsistent author-date ordering and formatting. Review each entry for uniform style.
Footer on page 2 lacks visible page numbering. Ensure all pages have consistent numbered footers.
OCR errors in footnotes introduce stray characters (e.g., â&lt;sup>â). Clean digital text to remove such artifacts.
Header displays the essay title and subject clearly, immediately orienting the reader to the Business Management focus.
The title precisely reflects the investigationâs focus on sustainability and brand loyalty, engaging the readerâs interest.
Define âgreenwashingâ when introducing it to ensure readers without prior knowledge can follow the argument.
Consider integrating survey data on consumer response to personalization versus sustainability to strengthen the customer experience section.
Acknowledging the limitation that Starbucks does not disclose spending data is valuable. Consider suggesting alternative dataâcollection approaches.
